name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BY PASS 77

surface_od_m.qualifier: as written (Ground level 75.80 m O D)

units: metres, transcribed verbatim from a typed Wimpey Geotech cable-percussion log (scale 1:50); depth = base below ground level

annotations: OD level of base: 75.30 m O D (checks: 75.80 - 0.50)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff mottled orange brown and grey sandy silty CLAY with some fine and medium angular to sub-rounded gravel
annotations: OD level of base: 72.80 m O D (checks: 75.80 - 3.00)

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense orange brown silty fine and medium SAND
annotations: OD level of base: 70.80 m O D (checks: 75.80 - 5.00)
water: 'DRY' recorded at casing 3.00m, then '3.10 GWe' and '4.50 GWe' logged near this base
with water: seepage of ground-water encountered overnight 23-24/3/90, level rose to a depth of 3.10m; cut off by the casing at a depth of 4.50m (per Remarks, sheet 4)

intervals.verbatim: Loose to medium dense brown clayey silty becoming very silty fine and medium SAND with bands of clayey silt throughout
annotations: OD level of base: 61.80 m O D (checks: 75.80 - 14.00)
water: '11.70 GWe' logged in this interval
with water: second seepage of ground-water encountered at a depth of 11.70m (per Remarks, sheet 4)

intervals.verbatim: Dense brown clayey silty fine to coarse SAND and fine to coarse angular to subrounded GRAVEL with some cobbles and boulders: layers of silty clay and bands of silty fine sand throughout
annotations: OD level of base: 45.35 m O D (checks: 75.80 - 30.45)
water: '13.20' / '14.50' logged in water-depth column at 24.95m (27/3)

borehole advanced by chiselling through this interval: 14.70-14.90m, 16.20-17.00m, 19.50-20.00m, 21.20-21.30m, 21.70-22.00m, 24.95-26.50m, 27.00-28.00m, 29.20-29.80m (per Remarks, sheet 4) — consistent with cobbles/boulders
END OF BOREHOLE at 30.45m; boring 200mm to 30.45m, casing 200mm to 26.40m; no bedrock reached

notes: Modern metric log (Wimpey Geotech, cable percussion, Dando 150), Sheet 1-4 of 4, Fig. 84; originator DJM 28/3/90, checked/approved AMcL 3/7/90; commenced 23/3/90. Entirely Quaternary drift (glacial / glaciofluvial): topsoil over sandy silty clay, sand, then a thick sequence of clayey silty sand and gravel with cobbles and boulders. NO bedrock, Coal Measures, or coal seams reached; hole terminated in the sand-and-gravel at 30.45m, index depth 30.45m matched exactly. Reduced levels (O D column) cross-check cleanly against ground level 75.80 m O D at every boundary (75.30, 72.80, 70.80, 61.80, 45.35) - no clerk arithmetic slips. No pencil seam marginalia present (drift-only hole). Handwritten 'NZ24SE/232' at the top of each sheet is the BGS accession/reference number, not log content. Groundwater: overnight seepage rose to 3.10m (23-24/3/90), cut off by casing at 4.50m; second seepage at 11.70m. Chiselling required repeatedly below 14.70m through the bouldery gravel. Full descriptions are typed and legible; all intervals confidence 'clear'.
